_header.purpose: Profiling dashboard for CPU and memory flame graph visualization with trace correlation
_header.scope: Continuous profiling analysis using Pyroscope datasource for CPU and memory profile types
_header.overview: Provides flame graph visualization for CPU and memory profiles collected by Pyroscope. Includes CPU flame graph panel, memory allocation flame graph panel, CPU profile over time, and instructions for differential flame graph analysis. Profiles can be correlated with traces when trace_id tags are present (configured via profile_with_trace_context in profiling.py). The Tempo datasource tracesToProfiles configuration enables direct navigation from trace spans to associated flame graphs.
_header.dependencies: Pyroscope datasource (uid: pyroscope), Tempo datasource (uid: tempo) for trace-to-profile links, profiling.py tag_wrapper for trace correlation
_header.interfaces: Pyroscope queries for process_cpu and memory profile types
_header.related: obs-summary.json for overview, web-traces.json for trace-to-profile correlation, profiling.py for Pyroscope SDK configuration
_header.implementation: Grafana dashboard JSON schema version 39 with flamegraph panels using Pyroscope datasource

title: Profiling
description: Where is the backend spending CPU time and allocating memory? Flame graphs show the full call stack — the wider a bar, the more time or memory that function consumes. Use the Service dropdown to select which service to profile. During a performance incident, start with the CPU flame graph to find the hot code path. For memory leaks, check the Memory Allocation flame graph for functions that allocate disproportionately. Profiles are automatically correlated with traces: from the Traces dashboard, click a slow span to jump directly to its flame graph.

panels.title: CPU Flame Graph
panels.description: Interactive CPU flame graph showing where the backend spends CPU time. Each bar is a function in the call stack — wider bars consume more CPU. Click any bar to zoom into that subtree. Look for wide bars that are not framework code — those are your optimization targets. During a CPU saturation incident, this panel directly shows the hot code path causing the problem.

panels.title: CPU Profile Over Time
panels.description: CPU profile intensity over time. Spikes correspond to periods of high CPU usage. Click on a time range in this panel and the flame graph above will update to show the profile for that period. Use this to find exactly when a CPU spike occurred and what code path caused it.

panels.title: Memory Allocation Flame Graph
panels.description: Interactive memory allocation flame graph showing which functions allocate the most objects. Wide bars at the top indicate high-allocation code paths. For memory leak investigations, compare this flame graph across two time periods using the Differential Flame Graph technique described in the guide below. Functions that grow wider over time are likely leaking.

panels.title: Memory Allocation Over Time
panels.description: Memory allocation volume over time. A steady baseline is normal. A rising trend may indicate a memory leak. Spikes correspond to bursts of object creation. Click a time range to update the Memory Allocation Flame Graph above to show which functions were allocating during that period.

options.content:
### Trace-to-Profile Correlation

Profiles are tagged with `trace_id` and `span_id` when the `profile_with_trace_context()` context manager is used in backend code. This enables:

1. **Trace -> Profile**: From the [Traces](/grafana/d/web-traces) dashboard, click a span to see its associated flame graph
2. **Profile Filtering**: Use Pyroscope label selector `{trace_id="<hex_id>"}` to find profiles for a specific trace

### Differential Flame Graphs

To compare profiles between two time periods:
1. Open **Explore** > Select **Pyroscope** datasource
2. Select profile type (CPU or Memory)
3. Use the **Diff** query type
4. Set baseline and comparison time ranges
5. Red areas = increased CPU/memory usage, Blue areas = decreased usage
